findings.summary: Capacity-waiting reconciliation executes before candidate selection and dispatch.
findings.evidence: Orchestrator.maybe_dispatch/1 reconciles running, blocked, and capacity_waiting state before fetching active issues and choosing dispatch candidates.

findings.summary: Remote release failure fails closed and retains local repository reservation.
findings.evidence: release_capacity_waiting_claim/4 removes claimed, capacity_waiting, and repository_claims state only after Tracker.release_issue/2 returns :ok; provider errors retain state.

findings.summary: Ownership transfer and valid-waiter recovery preserve the intended scheduler invariants.
findings.evidence: Foreign run ownership drops only stale local state without releasing the remote claim; valid owned waiters refresh and heartbeat while retaining queued attempt metadata and leaving retry_attempts unchanged.

findings.summary: Exact reviewed commit identity is affirmatively supported.
findings.evidence: Latest confirmed cycle-2 evidence records a clean local HEAD and tracking/live remote at 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36; an independent live compare reports bos/issue-51 identical to that SHA.

findings.summary: Merged-artifact installation and service health remain a post-merge delivery verification boundary.
findings.evidence: Existing exact-commit evidence identifies DeploymentVerification as outstanding after merge; this does not block the pre-merge architecture verdict.

summary: Passed architecture review for exact HEAD 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36. The design reconciles capacity waiters before dispatch, releases stale provider/local claims safely, preserves fencing on provider failure, respects claim ownership, and keeps valid waiting claims recoverable without Attempt or retry-budget consumption. Full exact-commit validation evidence is confirmed; post-merge installation and health verification remain delivery-coordinator responsibilities.
